creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
FYI: A separate note going in a different direction: what is acceptable upstream . . .

The new:

typedef int64_t off64_t;

would not be applied upstream. Why?

iozone targets spanning (I asked):

A) C89/C90 with the addition of %lld and long long (common extensions).
   (20+ year old compilers are deliberately targeted.)
B) C99 and C11 (which have %lld and long long in the standard).

[K&R C is not spanned.]

C89/C90 do not have int64_t and it is not a sufficiently common extension for 20+ year old compilers.

C99 and C11 only have int64_t as optional, not as required.

So this change can not be fed upstream without being rejected.
